findings.findingDetail: Detected a python logger call with a potential hardcoded secret "Pre token generation: injected role claim '%s' (trigger version %s)" being logged. This may lead to secret credentials being exposed. Make sure that the logger is not logging  sensitive information.

snippets.text:
logger.info(
        "Pre token generation: injected role claim '%s' (trigger version %s)",
        role_claim,
        version,
    )

findings.findingDetail:
Vulnerability: Enable S3 bucket access logging
Severity: INFORMATIONAL
Remediation: N/A

Vulnerability: Download of External Dependencies
Severity: INFORMATIONAL
Remediation: Remove the direct references to third party URLs as pointed by the finding.
The correct way to import 3rd party dependencies into our software is via appropriate Builder Tools vended tools  such as BrazilThirdPartyTool or Peru idiomatic tooling.
Refer https://policy.a2z.com/docs/25865/publication#5.2.2.1 for more details.

Please see the following: https://www.aristotle.a2z.com/recommendations/11 https://www.aristotle.a2z.com/recommendations/118 https://www.aristotle.a2z.com/recommendations/164

snippets.text:
echo "Cloning upstream MCP repository..."
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/awslabs/mcp.git
cd mcp/src/billing-cost-management-mcp-server
